description: Length-matched V2 facts for clinical translation domain. All length ratios 0.8-1.2x. Hedging removed, certainty balanced, distractors plausible but wrong.

facts.context: IND-enabling toxicology studies for gene therapy require:
facts.truth: GLP studies with clinical vector plus biodistribution
distractors: non-GLP range-finding studies with surrogate vector
computational QSAR modeling without in vivo testing
single-dose acute toxicity screens in rodent models

facts.context: Species selection for gene therapy tox studies depends on:
facts.truth: vector tropism, transgene expression, immune relevance
distractors: breeding cycle speed, colony cost, housing availability
genome size similarity to human reference chromosome
body mass ratio for simple allometric dose conversion

facts.context: Biodistribution studies for gene therapy assess:
facts.truth: vector DNA/RNA persistence and germline distribution
distractors: serum protein half-life within the first dosing week
transgene mRNA levels restricted to target organ only
capsid antigen clearance rate at the injection site

facts.context: Vector shedding studies for gene therapy evaluate:
facts.truth: vector excretion in body fluids for transmission risk
distractors: intracellular vector genome copy number at target site
viral capsid protein stability in standard cell culture
host immune response magnitude against vector antigens

facts.context: Potency assays for gene therapy products must:
facts.truth: measure biological activity tied to mechanism of action
distractors: quantify total viral genome copies by digital droplet PCR
assess vector particle-to-infectivity ratio by microscopy
determine capsid thermal stability by calorimetry methods

facts.context: Identity testing for AAV gene therapy confirms:
facts.truth: capsid serotype, transgene sequence, genome integrity
distractors: total protein content, osmolality value, buffer pH level
endotoxin burden, bioburden count, sterility test result
particle size distribution, viscosity, optical appearance

facts.context: Purity specifications for AAV vectors address:
facts.truth: empty capsid ratio, residual HCP/DNA, endotoxin
distractors: transgene codon usage, promoter strength, polyA signal
capsid thermal melting point and aggregation propensity
vector infectivity titer from standard transduction assay

facts.context: Manufacturing process changes require comparability showing:
facts.truth: equivalent quality, potency, and safety across changes
distractors: reduced per-dose production cost after scale optimization
shorter batch release timeline at the commercial facility
increased bioreactor yield from the new cell line clone

facts.context: Long-term follow-up for gene therapy patients exists because:
facts.truth: delayed adverse events require 5-15 year monitoring
distractors: acute infusion reactions resolve within initial 48 hours
vector genome integration stabilizes after the first month
neutralizing antibody titers peak within the first quarter

facts.context: Pediatric gene therapy trials have additional requirements:
facts.truth: growth effects, developing immunity, longer risk window
distractors: weight-based dosing adjustments using standard allometry
parental informed consent as the sole regulatory concern
smaller injection volumes scaled to body surface ratios

facts.context: Accelerated approval for gene therapies relies on:
distractors: preclinical efficacy from relevant animal disease model
Phase 1 safety data showing acceptable tolerability
manufacturing readiness confirmed at commercial scale

facts.context: Gene therapy pricing and reimbursement challenges arise from:
facts.truth: high upfront cost for curative one-time treatment
distractors: standard tiered pricing models from pharma formularies
annual subscription payments spread across fiscal years
volume discount structures negotiated by hospital groups
